frameworks.name: cognitive map
frameworks.one_line: Brain organizes concepts into a navigable map analogous to space.

frameworks.name: grid cell coding
frameworks.one_line: Hexagonally symmetric spatial code extended to non-spatial conceptual dimensions.

core_question: Do humans use a hexagonally symmetric grid-like code to navigate conceptual relations between objects defined by two continuous dimensions?
key_assumptions: Concepts can be represented in a 2-D continuous space
Grid-like hexagonal symmetry in fMRI signal indexes grid-cell-like coding
Conceptual navigation engages mechanisms analogous to spatial navigation
manipulations: Trajectory angle through a 2-D conceptual space defined by two continuous object dimensions
Similarity judgments along these conceptual trajectories
key_findings: Entorhinal cortex showed hexagonal-symmetry signature modulated by trajectory angle
Ventromedial prefrontal cortex also exhibited the predicted hexagonal symmetry
interpretations: Humans deploy a grid-like code for non-spatial, conceptual cognition
The same hexagonal coding mechanism supports both spatial and conceptual navigation
